title: Route
description: A named, addressable destination in the application's navigation graph, representing a URL path pattern and the view or component it resolves to. Routes are the atomic unit of the navigation layer — each route maps a URL pattern to a specific UX artifact and carries an access classification (public, protected, redirect, alias, or lazy-loaded) that governs how the router handles incoming requests. Routes may be composed into NavigationFlows to model multi-step user journeys.

type.description: Access and loading classification: public (no auth), protected (requires auth), redirect (auto-forwards), alias (multiple paths to same component), lazy (code-split, loaded on demand).
